clusters.title: Virginia Supreme Court Strikes Down Democratic Redistricting Plan
clusters.summary: The Virginia Supreme Court has invalidated a newly drawn redistricting map that favored Democrats, significantly impacting their prospects in the upcoming midterm elections. This decision is seen as a setback for the party, as it alters the electoral landscape ahead of crucial contests. The ruling underscores ongoing debates over redistricting practices and their implications for political representation in the state.

clusters.title: Spain Evacuates Cruise Ship Amid Hantavirus Outbreak
clusters.summary: Spain is coordinating the evacuation of passengers from a cruise ship experiencing a hantavirus outbreak, with several European countries sending planes to assist. The outbreak has raised health concerns as it unfolded over several weeks before being identified. Authorities are working to ensure the safety of those on board and to contain the spread of the virus.

clusters.title: U.S. Strikes Iranian Tankers Amid Escalating Iran-UAE Tensions
clusters.summary: U.S. fighter jets targeted two Iranian oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, citing violations of a blockade, while Iran launched missile and drone attacks aimed at the United Arab Emirates. The UAE reported successful interceptions of these attacks, raising concerns about the stability of the ongoing ceasefire in the region. This escalation highlights the increasing tensions between the U.S., Iran, and their regional allies.

clusters.title: Péter Magyar Sworn in as Hungary's New Prime Minister
clusters.summary: Péter Magyar has been officially sworn in as Hungary's new prime minister, marking the end of Viktor Orbán's 16-year tenure. His Tisza party achieved a significant parliamentary majority, leading to widespread celebrations in Budapest. This transition is seen as a pivotal moment in Hungary's political landscape, signaling a shift in governance and policy direction.

items.title: Hungary's new PM sworn in during 'regime change' party
items.description: Nearly a month ago Péter Magyar steered his Tisza party to a landslide victory, sweeping away 16 years of rule by Viktor Orbán.

items.title: Peter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s PM, ending Orban’s 16 years in power
items.description: Magyar's Tisza party has secured a huge parliamentary majority.

items.description: Magyar sworn in as Hungary's prime minister  Reuters

items.title: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s prime minister to end 16-year Orbán era
items.description:
Jubilation in Budapest as Péter Magyar becomes leader and invites people to ‘step through gate of regime change’

• Europe live – latest updates

The pro-European centre-right leader Péter Magyar has been sworn in as prime minister of Hungary, marking the official end to Viktor Orbán’s 16 years in power.

Saturday’s ceremony – during which Magyar had invited people to join him to “write Hungarian history” together and “step through the gate of regime change” – comes a month after his opposition Tisza party won a landslide victory in parliamentary elections.

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Putin Leads Scaled-Back Victory Day Parade Amid Ukraine Tensions
clusters.summary: Russia held a downsized Victory Day parade, with President Vladimir Putin emphasizing the significance of the Soviet victory in World War II while addressing ongoing military efforts in Ukraine. The event, marked by reduced attendance and military displays, reflects heightened concerns over the conflict's impact on national morale and security. This year's observance comes as the war in Ukraine continues to escalate, prompting varied reactions domestically and internationally.

clusters.title: Ted Turner, CNN Founder and Media Pioneer, Passes Away at 87
clusters.summary: Ted Turner, the influential media mogul and founder of CNN, has died at the age of 87. Known for pioneering 24-hour news coverage, Turner significantly transformed the media landscape and left a lasting impact on journalism and broadcasting. His legacy includes not only CNN but also his contributions to environmental causes and philanthropy.

clusters.title: Frontier Airlines Flight Hits Person During Takeoff at Denver Airport
clusters.summary: A Frontier Airlines plane struck a person on the runway during takeoff at Denver International Airport, leading to an engine fire and the need for an emergency evacuation. All 224 passengers and seven crew members were safely evacuated from the aircraft. Authorities are currently investigating the incident and managing the ongoing emergency response.

items.title: Frontier Jet Hits Person on Runway During Takeoff at Denver Airport
items.description: The plane reported an engine fire and aborted its takeoff. Those on board were safely evacuated.

items.title: Frontier Airlines plane hits person on runway during takeoff at Denver airport
items.description:
Passengers were evacuated safely after the plane struck a person and an engine then caught on fire

A Frontier Airlines plane hit a person on the runway of Denver’s international airport during takeoff, sparking an engine fire and forcing passengers to evacuate, authorities said.

The plane, headed to Los Angeles, “reported striking a pedestrian during takeoff” at about 11.19pm on Friday, the Denver airport’s official X account wrote.

Continue reading...

items.title: Frontier Plane Aborts Takeoff After Hitting a Person on the Runway
items.description: A Frontier Airlines plane had to abort its takeoff after hitting a person on the runway at Denver International Airport. The airline says all 224 passengers and seven crew members evacuated safely from the plane. The airport says the emergency response is ongoing and the runway where the pedestrian was struck will remain closed as the investigation continues. NBC’s Valerie Castro reports for Saturday TODAY.

clusters.title: US Military Strike in Eastern Pacific Kills Two on Drug Boat
clusters.summary: A U.S. military strike targeting an alleged drug-trafficking boat in the eastern Pacific Ocean resulted in the deaths of two individuals, with one survivor reported. This incident marks the third military action in the region in May, highlighting ongoing efforts to combat drug trafficking. The strike raises questions about the tactics and implications of U.S. operations in international waters.

clusters.title: Evidence of Human Remains Found in Kristin Smart Case
clusters.summary: Soil testing at a California residence linked to Paul Flores, who was convicted of murdering college student Kristin Smart in 1996, has revealed evidence suggesting the presence of human remains. This development comes as authorities continue to investigate the circumstances surrounding Smart's disappearance and the implications for the ongoing legal proceedings. The case remains a significant focus for both law enforcement and the community, highlighting unresolved issues from the past.

items.title: Soil testing at California house turns up evidence of human remains in Kirstin Smart case
items.description:
Home reportedly occupied by mother of Paul Flores, who was convicted of killing college student who went missing in 1996

Soil testing at a property linked to the man convicted in the murder of California college student Kristin Smart, who disappeared in 1996, turned up evidence of human remains, a state sheriff announced on Friday.

“We can’t call it Kristin, but there’s evidence to support human remains – there at one time,” the San Luis Obispo county sheriff, Ian Parkinson, said at a news conference.

Continue reading...

items.title: Evidence points to human remains at home linked to Kristin Smart's killing, sheriff says
items.description: A California sheriff says that evidence suggests human remains were present at a home connected to the man convicted of killing 19-year-old college student Kristin Smart in 1996

clusters.title: Two Men Charged for Filming Antisemitic TikTok Videos in London
clusters.summary: Adam Bedoui, 20, and Abdelkader Amir Bousloub, 21, have been charged with religiously aggravated harassment after allegedly filming antisemitic TikTok videos in north London. The charges follow reports of a hate crime, and the men are scheduled to appear in magistrates' court on Saturday. This incident highlights ongoing concerns regarding hate speech and antisemitism on social media platforms.

items.title: Two men charged over alleged filming of antisemitic TikTok videos in London
items.description:
Adam Bedoui, 20, and Abdelkader Amir Bousloub, 21, charged with religiously aggravated harassment

Two men have been charged with religiously aggravated harassment over allegations they travelled to north London to film antisemitic TikTok videos.

Officers were called to reports of a hate crime involving a group of men allegedly harassing members of the Jewish community on Clapton Common at about 9pm on Thursday, the Metropolitan police said. Officers arrested five men in Hackney after the incident, it added.

Continue reading...

items.title: Police charge two men over filming antisemitic TikToks in London
items.description: Adam Bedoui, 20, and Abdelkader Amir Bousloub, 21, are due to appear at magistrates' court on Saturday.

clusters.title: Judge Rules DOGE's $100M Cuts to Humanities Funding Illegal
clusters.summary: A federal judge has ruled that the Department of Education's Office of General Counsel (DOGE) acted illegally by using artificial intelligence to identify $100 million in cuts to the National Endowment for the Humanities. The decision highlights concerns over the use of AI in budgetary decisions and its implications for federal funding in the humanities sector. This ruling may prompt a reevaluation of how federal agencies implement budget cuts and the methods employed in such processes.

This development highlights the ongoing discussions about the judiciary's independence and its impact on American law.
